title: Delivery Tags, Acknowledgements, and Redelivery · Go Semantics Case: Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must keep delivery tags scoped to the owning channel generation and choose exactly one Ack, Nack, or Reject after effect evidence; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the manual auto ack tradeoff increment in a same-channel terminal delivery decision record. Lear…
objectives: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Choose acknowledgement, requeue, dead-letter, or channel-close behavior for in-flight work during graceful and forced shutdown; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff · Theory Manual Auto Ack Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4535 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
